name: command
summary: CLI command exposed to operators with a signature, description, and handle() entry point.
description: Creates a CLI command and registers it in an initializer
problem: You want operators (developers, deploy scripts, ops staff) to trigger an action from the terminal — running a migration, syncing data, kicking off a one-off process — with proper argument parsing and help output.
appliesWhen: An operator needs to trigger this action from the terminal
The action benefits from arguments and options with help text
It runs to completion in one shot (not a long-running daemon)
It's a maintenance, ops, or data task — not user-facing
avoidWhen: End users will trigger this — use phpnomad/controller for HTTP
It runs continuously or in response to events — use phpnomad/task
It's invoked programmatically by other PHP code — make it a plain service
It's a one-time script that won't be re-run — just write the script
examples: Create a CLI tool to sync subscriptions
Add a console command that backfills order data
I need a wp-cli command to clear a cache
tradeoffs: One class per command keeps each focused but produces more files than a single big script. Argument parsing handled via the signature DSL — straightforward but learn-once.
outputs: A {name} command class with getSignature(), getDescription(), and handle() methods
A registration adding the command to the initializer's getCommands() list
postApply: Implement handle() with the actual command logic
Test by running `<your-cli-binary> <command-name>` after composer dump-autoload

name.aiHint: PascalCase, ending in 'Command'. Class name describes what it does (DeployCommand, BackfillOrdersCommand).

signature.aiHint: First token is the command name (kebab-case). Use {arg:description} for arguments, {--option=default:description} for options. Trailing ? on an arg makes it optional.

description.example: Deploy the application to a target environment
description.aiHint: One sentence, imperative. Shown when listing commands. Don't repeat the command name.

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register in
initializer.aiHint: Pick the initializer that owns related commands. If unsure, use the project's root initializer.
